source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A point light source is located behind a transparent sphere. The paraxial light rays emitted from the front surface of the sphere are parallel as shown in figure. Find the refractive index of the transparent sphere material.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'as shown in figure'
the needed optical geometry (source position relative to sphere and ray behavior) is not fully specified in text alone
asset_registry_record shows image exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: solving requires combining optics concepts with the depicted geometry, not just direct lookup
the condition that emergent paraxial rays from the front surface are parallel implies an intermediate/focal relation that must be inferred
finding refractive index requires at least two linked reasoning steps: interpret the diagram setup, then apply refraction/paraxial relations
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='2', which is easy to compare against a derived result
multiple-choice options are provided, enabling straightforward final-answer checking
registry_passed=true, answer_present=true, and no asset issues are listed
the underlying physics derivation is formula-based, so intermediate reasoning can be checked if needed

decision_reasons: question, image, and numeric answer are all present, so the core semantics are intact
asset registry passed with no listed issues
image-dependent geometry is supported because the required figure exists
